project.title: circMAN1A2_009-pulled down proteins in HeLa cells
project.projectDescription: CircRNA pulldown assays were conducted to identify proteins interacting with circMAN1A2_009. In summary, HeLa cell protein extracts were incubated with biotin-labeled circMAN1A2_009 probes obtained from Tsingke Biotechnology, China, at room temperature for 1h. Subsequently, streptavidin magnetic beads were introduced into the cell lysates containing the probes and incubated overnight at 4°C. After three washes, proteins bound to the streptavidin magnetic beads were eluted using the Elution Buffer as per the instruction provided by the PierceTM Magnetic RNA-Protein Pull-Down Kit (Thermo, 20164, USA). The retrieved proteins underwent analysis through liquid chromatography-tandem mass spectrometry (LS-MS/MS) conducted by OE biotech Co. Ltd (Shanghai, China), or Western Blot.
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Proteins eluted from the RNA pull down assay was resolved by 10% SDS-PAGE gel. After PAGE electrophoresis, the gel was silver stained (solarbio, G7210). The gel with obvious different bands was excised and digested. After protein digestion and peptide desalination, the enzymolysis products were separated by capillary high performance liquid chromatography and analyzed by Q-Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific).
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Corrected by standard correction solution, parent ion scanning range: 300-1500 m/z. Mass spectrum scanning mode was Data Dependent Aqcuisition. The strongest 20 fragment atlas (MS2 scan) was collected after each full scan. Fragmentation mode: high energy collision dissociation, NCE energy 28, dynamic exclusion time: 25s. The MS1 resolution was 70,000 at M/Z 200, the AGC target was set to 3e6, and the maximum injection time was 100 ms. The MS2 resolution was set to 17500, the AGC target was set to 1e5, and the maximum injection time is 50 ms. Mass spectra analysis utilized Mascot 2.3 (Matrix Science) with the UniProtKB human database (Uniport Homo sapiens 188441_20200326; RRID: SCR_004426).
